documents.label: A copy of the minor's birth certificate
A copy of your birth certificate
A court order
Foster care certification
A copy of your driver's license or other government-issued identification
A lawfully executed and valid power of attorney
A copy of the minor's Social Security card
A copy of your Social Security card

authority.notes: Listed under 'To show you are the minor's parent or authorized representative' — any one of these is sufficient.

parentId.notes: Listed under 'For proof of your identity' with 'provide one from each category' — the bullets within this category are OR options.

address.notes: The form does not list a separate proof-of-address document category; the guardian's current address is collected on the form itself.

childId.notes: The form explicitly says 'For the minor, please provide BOTH of the following' — both the minor's Social Security card and birth certificate are required.

documents.label: Child's birth certificate
Proof of guardianship (if not named on child's birth certificate)
Driver's license or another government issued identification card (e.g. state ID card)
Child's Social Security card
Proof of address (bank statement, utility bill, or insurance statement)

authority.notes: The child's birth certificate establishes parentage/authority if the parent is named on it. If the parent is not named on the birth certificate, proof of guardianship (court order) is required instead. These are mutually exclusive alternatives depending on the situation.

childId.notes: The page explicitly lists both 'a copy of your child's birth certificate' AND 'a copy of your child's Social Security card' as required items with no indication that one can substitute for the other. Both are required.

mailingAddress.notes: The page lists two addresses: standard mail (PO Box 9554) and overnight mail (701 Experian Parkway). The standard mail address is the primary mailing address. Note: this page covers checking if a minor has a credit report; the same address is used for minor-related requests including security freezes per Experian's general minor request page.

documents.label: A document issued by a federal, state, or local government agency showing proof of parentage, including a birth certificate
Certified or official copy of a birth certificate issued by the entity authorized to issue the birth certificate (parent)
An order issued by a court of law
Written communication from a county welfare/probation department certifying foster care placement
A guardianship or conservator document that is current and not expired, includes the protected consumer's name, and the name matches the person submitting the request
documents.description: Guardianship or conservatorship document naming the requestor as guardian/conservator of the protected consumer

documents.label: Copy of a driver's license, state-issued identification card, or any other government-issued identification (minor)
Copy of a driver's license, state-issued identification card, or any other government-issued identification (parent)
A lawfully executed and valid power of attorney
Social Security number or a copy of a Social Security card (minor)
Social Security number or a copy of a Social Security card (parent)

authority.notes: The page requires one piece of documentation proving sufficient proof of authority. Guardianship/conservator documents are grouped under 'court' for the widget display.

parentId.notes: The page groups parent and child identity documents together under one 'sufficient proof of identification' section, listing SSN/card, birth certificate, and government-issued ID as options. Any one is sufficient for the parent.

address.notes: The page does not list a proof-of-address requirement for the protected consumer freeze request.

childId.notes: The page requires 'sufficient proof of identification' for both the parent AND the minor. The same document types apply: SSN/card, birth certificate, or government-issued ID. The child's birth certificate (birth_child) also appears in the authority category.

mailingAddress.notes: This address is explicitly labeled on the page as 'the dedicated mailing address for protected consumer freezes at TransUnion.' It is distinct from the standard freeze removal address (P.O. Box 160, Woodlyn, PA 19094).
